page: Amazon Web Services u7in-32tb.224xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance designed for workloads requiring massive memory footprints. Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance features 896 dedicated vCPUs built on the x86_64 architecture using Intel processors. The physical core count stands at 448, with 2.0 threads per core running at a clock speed of 1.9 GHz.

The defining characteristic of this instance is its memory capacity, offering 32,768.0 GB of RAM, which translates to approximately 36.57 GB of memory per core. It does not include local NVMe storage or onboard GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. To support this, the instance is network and EBS optimized, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps.

Given its hardware profile, this instance is designed for large-scale enterprise applications. The massive memory capacity and high vCPU count make it suitable for running large in-memory databases, massive SAP HANA deployments, real-time big data analytics, and memory-intensive enterprise resource planning workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services u7in-32tb.224xlarge is an ultra-high-memory instance designed for workloads requiring massive RAM capacity. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this x86_64 architecture instance is powered by Intel processors with 896 dedicated vCPUs across 448 physical cores running at 1.9 GHz. It features an exceptional 32,768.0 GB of system memory, providing 36.57 GB of RAM per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPUs, relying instead on network-attached storage supported by a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This configuration is optimized for network and EBS performance, making it highly efficient for memory-bound enterprise applications. It is best suited for massive in-memory databases, large-scale SAP HANA installations, and heavy real-time data processing workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u7in-32tb.224xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 896 Intel vCPUs, 32 TB of RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for massive in-memory database workloads.
